danger_signals.visual_form: Battle screen transition; enemies appear on left side of screen in combat layout
danger_signals.meaning: Danger present on current map; player must fight or flee

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual state of crystals in story/dialogue, implied through narrative and map areas
danger_signals.meaning: World corruption by Elemental Fiends; danger to world stability

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct tileset areas: forests, caves, mountains, swamps, underwater caverns, buildings
danger_signals.meaning: Hazardous locations containing enemies and treasures; progression-gated danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Preset battle locations with named enemies (e.g., Lich, Fiend of Earth)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat; story-critical danger requiring specific preparation

safety_signals.visual_form: Safe map areas with NPCs, shops, and dialogue boxes
safety_signals.meaning: No random encounters; player can rest, buy supplies, gather information

safety_signals.visual_form: Pause menu allowing inventory, equipment, magic, and level tracking
safety_signals.meaning: Safe state; player can manage resources and review progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Character stats displayed in menu showing current health and level
safety_signals.meaning: Player capability assessment; visual confirmation of party strength

safety_signals.visual_form: Sprite-based ship, canoe, and airship on overworld map
safety_signals.meaning: Progression unlocked; new safe routes and fast travel available

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crystal locations; dungeons containing elemental themes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Named boss enemies (Lich - Fiend of Earth; implied others for Fire, Water, Wind)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Story-referenced crystal corruption; world map shows dungeons with Fiend presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased random encounters; NPCs in towns express distress; dungeons become accessible as threats are confronted

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite portraits mature during class upgrades; new equipment and weapons appear visually on characters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stronger enemies encountered; new transportation options unlocked (ship, canoe, airship); previously inaccessible areas become traversable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Menu screen shows rising experience levels (1-50 range); increased HP and stat values; upgraded weapons and armor equippable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Battle outcomes shift in player favor; random encounters become less threatening; boss encounters become viable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All characters reach zero HP; game over screen appears
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progress lost; return to last save; world state reverts

environmental_storytelling.example: Different dungeon types (forests, caves, mountains, swamps, underwater, buildings) reflect elemental/thematic narrative; placement on overworld reveals story progression path
Town citizens offer helpful information and hints about dungeon locations, enemy types, and story progression; dialogue conveys world state anxiety about Fiends
Different shops in different towns offer progressive equipment tiers; availability reflects player advancement and dungeon completion
Rare items found only in dungeon chests, not in shops; signals dungeon importance and rewards exploration

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Ship, canoe, airship unlock sequentially on overworld map
environmental_storytelling.example: Progressive unlocking of travel methods (foot → ship → canoe → airship) visualizes world opening and narrative progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initiate turn-based combat; can be fought or fled from; yield experience and Gil on defeat
Preset battle locations; require specific preparation; upon defeat, contribute to crystal restoration narrative
Offer dialogue hints and information; shopkeepers provide buy/sell transactions; behavior varies based on story state
Combat actions vary by class (Fighter attacks, Mages cast spells, Thief uses special abilities); visual maturation during class upgrade reflects capability increase

notes: Final Fantasy (1987) uses a simplified but effective visual communication system for an 8-bit RPG. The four-youth Warriors of Light quest structure against four Elemental Fiends creates a symmetrical narrative framework. Visual communication relies heavily on menu/stat displays, dungeon environment variety, and overworld map design rather than detailed sprite work. Elemental crystal corruption serves as both mechanical goal indicator and thematic centerpiece. The game pioneered several visual conventions: left-side enemy placement in combat, character sprite side on right, level-based stat progression visualization. Transportation unlocks (ship → canoe → airship) create clear visual milestones of world state change and player capability progression.
